title: Reading is a System
subtitle: Read more widely, deeply, and effectively by treating reading as a system. Try building a cart with strategies that appeal to your goals.

strategies.title: Parallel Reading
strategies.subtitle: Read several sources at once to expose shared claims, tensions, and gaps.
strategies.body: Reading multiple books at once allows you to unlock many benifits that you would not get from reading one book at a time. You'll learn to discursively read, to make reading sessions more consistent, and to drop books caught in sunk cost fallacies.

youtubeLinks.label: You Should Read Multiple Books at Once
How to read multiple books at once - GlutenbergBible
Reading Multiple Books at the Same Time - Mike Nowhere

strategies.title: You Won't Remember Everything You Read
strategies.subtitle: Make a Lookup Table to store and search for information you want to remember.
strategies.body: Our brains are not designed to remember everything we read. Instead, we should focus on remembering the information that is most relevant to our goals. A Lookup Table is a simple tool that allows you to store and search for information you want to remember.

youtubeLinks.label: You Can't Remember Everything You Read So Don't Bother

strategies.title: Commonplace Book
strategies.subtitle: A shared platform for storing and searching for information you want to remember.
strategies.body: A commonplace book is your personal knowledge management system. It is a shared platform for storing and searching for information you want to remember across all of your reading. It is a tool that allows you to capture and organize your thoughts, ideas, and insights in a way that is meaningful to you.

strategies.title: Check In Electronic Devices
strategies.subtitle: VPNs and blockers don't work in the throes of active distraction. Check in your devices to a different room to avoid the temptation.

strategies.title: Question Based Reading
strategies.subtitle: Don't start reading a book without questions in mind. Use questions as a detective to guide your reading and to help you remember what you read.

youtubeLinks.label: Oxford Study Skills: How to Read

strategies.title: iOS Questions Shortcut
strategies.subtitle: A shortcut for iOS that allows you to quickly revisit your current books with a random question.

strategies.title: Entity Shorthand
strategies.subtitle: A method of quickly storing bookmarks for later reference.

strategies.title: Start Skimming
strategies.subtitle: Not all books are meant to be read cover to cover. Skim the parts that are less relevant to your goals.

assets.label: How to Read a Book by Mortimer J. Adler and Charles Van Doren

strategies.title: Different Books Call for Different Reading Strategies
strategies.subtitle: No two books are written the same, and no two books should be read the same. Adjust your reading strategy to the book you're reading.

strategies.title: Always Be Reading
strategies.subtitle: Have a bit of reading in your daily life whenever possible. The practice is more important than the destination.

strategies.title: How Readers Do Chores Around the House
strategies.subtitle: Learn how to use audiobooks while doing chores and set a sleep timer to reflect on what you've learned.

youtubeLinks.label: Get a Cleaner Home and Better Memory At The Same Time

strategies.title: Spaced Repetition
strategies.subtitle: Use Anki to remember what you read. Spaced repetition is a method of reviewing information at increasing intervals to help you remember it better.

strategies.title: Phone Foyer Method
strategies.subtitle: Leave your phone plugged in in the kitchen or foyer to avoid the temptation of checking it.

youtubeLinks.label: The Anxiety Generation Machine Of Phone Use Before Bed- Cal Newport

strategies.title: Reading Shouldn't Be A Punishment
strategies.subtitle: Don't moralize reading. Reading is a skill that can be learned and improved upon, and it should be enjoyable.
strategies.body: Often people will only brag about reading books that are difficult or popular as a form of social signaling. Read what brings you joy, and don't feel bad about reading books that are easy.

strategies.title: The Total Sum of Books You'll Ever Read is a Tiny Fraction of the Total Sum of Books You Could Read
The Ergonomics of Digital Books, Analog Books, and Audiobooks
Builder Spender Reading
strategies.subtitle: Building (Active Reading): Set a timer for 10-minute intervals to read (or listen) to a book. Spending (The Mental Dump): Once the timer goes off, immediately capture your thoughts in a commonplace book.

youtubeLinks.label: How World of Warcraft Made Me a Better Reader

strategies.title: Finishing the Book Before it Ends

youtubeLinks.label: I've never had a reading slump, here's how - Horse Jaw

strategies.title: Say the Subject First, Then Cite if Asked
Make YouTube Playlists for Each Book You Read
Buy Physical Copies of Five Star Books You Read Digitally
Vibe Code Static Sites for Interactive Book Reviews
strategies.subtitle: This very website is an example of a static site about books!

strategies.title: Only the First Few Minutes Will Feel Like Work
Time to Novelty (Competing with Short Form Content)
Stop in the Middle, Not a Good Spot to Stop
Think About Books That Are Not In Front of You
strategies.subtitle: Use Post-it notes or phone wallpapers to remind yourself of books you want to read or books you are currently reading.

strategies.title: Create While You Read
strategies.subtitle: Create something while you read to help you remember what you read and to share your insights with others. Art, music, writing, and code are all great ways to create while you read.
